skill.name: firebase-remote-config-basics
skill.description: Comprehensive guide for Firebase Remote Config, including template management and SDK usage. Use this skill when the user needs help setting up Remote Config, managing feature flags, or updating app behavior dynamically.
skill.summary: Official Firebase skill for setting up Remote Config, managing feature flags, and deploying config templates via CLI on Android and iOS.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 69 static findings are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code blocks containing legitimate bash CLI commands (npx firebase-tools). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are version numbers like firebase-bom:32.7.0. The 'hardcoded URL' findings reference official Firebase and Google documentation URLs. The 'system reconnaissance' matches are common words like 'config' and 'login' in a documentation context. No prompt injection, no malicious code, no data exfiltration patterns detected.

content.user_title: Set up Firebase Remote Config for Android and iOS
content.value_statement: Developers need a reliable way to manage feature flags and dynamic app behavior without releasing updates. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for integrating Firebase Remote Config, managing templates via CLI, and following best practices for fetching and activating values.
actual_capabilities: Guides Firebase project and app creation via the Firebase CLI
Explains how to add Remote Config and Analytics dependencies in Android Gradle and iOS Swift Package Manager
Demonstrates setting in-app default values using XML and plist files
Shows how to fetch and activate Remote Config values in both Kotlin and Swift
Documents template management workflows including get, edit, and deploy via CLI
Provides troubleshooting steps for common npx and project context errors
limitations: Covers only Android and iOS platforms; web and Unity setup is not included
Does not handle server-side Remote Config or REST API usage
Requires the user to have or create a Firebase project and app
CLI deployment commands depend on network access to the Firebase backend

use_cases.title: Integrate Remote Config in a new Android app
use_cases.description: A mobile developer wants to add feature flags to their Android app using Firebase Remote Config. The skill walks through project setup, Gradle dependencies, default values, and fetch-and-activate patterns.

use_cases.title: Deploy a Remote Config template via CLI
use_cases.description: A DevOps engineer needs to update feature flags in production without an app release. The skill explains how to get, edit, review, and deploy the remote_config.json template using the Firebase CLI.

use_cases.title: Set up Remote Config for iOS using Swift Package Manager
use_cases.description: An iOS developer wants to integrate Remote Config into their Swift project. The skill covers bundle ID discovery, Firebase app registration, SDK installation via SPM, and initialization code.

prompt_templates.title: Initial setup for Android
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner starting a new Android project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firebase Remote Config in my Android app. I already have a Firebase project and Android app registered. Walk me through adding the Gradle dependencies and initializing the SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Set in-app default values
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a remote_config_defaults.xml file with default values for welcome_message and is_feature_enabled, and show me the Kotlin code to load these defaults on app startup.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch and activate values
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Kotlin code to fetch the latest Remote Config template, activate it, and retrieve the welcome_message string value with proper success and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a template update
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current Remote Config template, update the is_feature_enabled parameter to true for 50% of users using a conditional rollout, and deploy the change after I review it.

output_examples.input: Create a Gradle dependencies block for Firebase Remote Config and Analytics using the Firebase BoM.
output_examples.output: Add the Firebase BoM and the Remote Config and Analytics libraries to your app-level build.gradle.kts. The BoM manages versions so you only specify the library names without versions.

output_examples.input: Show me how to fetch and activate Remote Config values in Kotlin.
output_examples.output: Use fetchAndActivate() to retrieve the latest template from the Firebase backend and apply it immediately. Handle the result in a completion listener to check for success and access the parameter values.

output_examples.input: Deploy a Remote Config template change via the CLI.
output_examples.output: First, get the current template and save it locally. Edit the JSON file to change parameters or conditions. After user review, create a firebase.json mapping and run the deploy command scoped to remoteconfig only.

best_practices: Always set in-app default values so the app functions correctly before the first fetch or when offline.
Activate previously fetched values on startup and fetch new values in the background to minimize user wait time.
Require explicit user review of template changes before deploying to production.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy Remote Config template changes without user verification of the modified JSON file.
Do not block the app UI while waiting for fetchAndActivate to complete; use background fetching patterns.
Do not hardcode feature flag values in source code when Remote Config is available for dynamic control.

faq.question: What is Firebase Remote Config used for?
faq.answer: Firebase Remote Config lets you change your app's behavior and appearance remotely without publishing an app update, using a cloud-based configuration template.

faq.question: Which platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers Android and iOS. It includes Gradle setup for Android and Swift Package Manager setup for iOS.

faq.question: Do I need the Firebase CLI?
faq.answer: The Firebase CLI is recommended for template management and deployment. The skill instructs Claude to use npx to run firebase-tools if it is not installed globally.

faq.question: How do I deploy a template change?
faq.answer: Get the current template with remoteconfig:get, edit the local JSON file, get user approval, then run firebase deploy with the remoteconfig scope.

faq.question: What happens if the app is offline?
faq.answer: In-app default values defined in XML or plist files ensure the app has functional configuration even when the device cannot reach the Firebase backend.

faq.question: Can I use Remote Config for A/B testing?
faq.answer: Remote Config supports conditional rollouts using conditions like percent randomization and user properties. For full experimentation, integrate Google Analytics as recommended in the skill.
